Government of Maharashtra
Department of Agriculture, Animal Husbandry, Dairying Business and Fisheries
GOVERNMENT RESOLUTION NUMBER-FUVIO-2026 / PRQ र.97/11-अ E / EO No.1641401
Madam Cama Marg, Martyrdom Rajguru Chowk,
Mantralaya, Mumbai - 400 032
Date: June 11, 2026
Read-
The Proposal -
Financial stability of farmers by providing insurance cover to their fruit crops against weather hazards.
Priority under the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana in the state is to provide assistance from the point of view of sustainability.
A restructured climate-based Fertility Insurance Scheme is being implemented. Due to various weather hazards,
The productivity of the fruit is adversely affected, leading to a large-scale decline in production.
The farmers face a financial crisis as they do not get the expected yield.
Restructured Weather Based Fruit Insurance Scheme under Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana, in the State
In the 2 years AD 2024-25 and AD 2025-26, the antelope population includes oranges, cucumbers, pomegranates, chickpeas, guava, lemons,
Indian Agriculture Insurance with Revenue Board component in 26 districts for 8 fruits, namely, Citrus and Grapefruit (C)
Companies, Bajaj General Insurance Limited, Generali Central Insurance Company Limited and Universal Sompo General
The insurance company is being operated by these insurance companies under the Government Decision in Reference No. (1).
Remaining State share under the Structured Climate Based Fruit Insurance Scheme Mrig Bahar 2025
For subsidy, the Commissionerate of Agriculture has sanctioned an amount of Rs. 11,42,40, 911 / - (Rs.) to the insurance companies under Reference No. (5).
11 crore forty-two lakh forty-four thousand nine hundred and eleven however) requested to be disbursed.
Accordingly, under the restructured climate-based Fruit and Vegetable Insurance Scheme, Mrig Bahar will be available for the rest of the year 2025.
